title: Shared empty strategy result exposes mutable Set instances
reasoning: The early-exit result is a module-level singleton and the comment correctly notes that mutating its inner Sets would leak state across callers. However Object.freeze only freezes the wrapper object, while ProbedStrategies exposes mutable Set<string> values; even with Readonly<ProbedStrategies>, TypeScript still permits result.cdpPoolIds.add(...). Any caller that mutates an empty result from a no-RPC or no-rebalancer network can pollute all future empty results from this module.
recommendation: Do not return a shared object containing mutable Sets. Either allocate fresh empty Sets on each early exit, or change the public type to ReadonlySet<string> and enforce immutability with fresh results or a genuinely immutable wrapper.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests do not exercise detectProbedStrategies. Tests that only assert empty set sizes would also miss this unless they mutate one returned result and then call an early-exit path again.
suggestedRegressionTest: Call detectProbedStrategies with a network missing rpcUrl, mutate result.cdpPoolIds, call it again with the same no-RPC network, and assert the second result is still empty.
minimumFixScope: ui-dashboard/src/lib/strategy-detection.ts and a focused regression test for early-exit result isolation.

history.note: Fixed by returning fresh empty strategy result sets for early exits; added a mutation-isolation regression.
